courseleaf.title: Physics, Master of Science
courseleaf.informationtext:
Graduate Study

The Department of Physics & Astronomy offers programs of study and research leading to M.S. and Ph.D. degrees. For further information regarding requirements, graduate research, teaching assistantships, and research assistantships, see Physics Graduate Group.

Degree Requirements & Program Coordinator

See Physics.
courseleaf.quickviewtext: Come study a broad range of experimental and theoretical research interests. The physics program provides a dynamic and diverse environment for graduate students with efforts in condensed matter physics, cosmology, and high energy/elementary particle physics. We have smaller groups studying biophysics, gravitation, nuclear physics, and physics education.  Most students continue on to physics jobs in industry, academia, or at national labs.
